logs.heading: A0: Loading gmail skill to fetch and cache email threads

thoughts: Found the 'gmail' skill (v3.2.0) in the list - it reads Gmail email threads using Chrome DevTools, caches messages in SQLite, and outputs summarized results.
The user wants me to load this skill and let it run autonomously for 5-15 minutes without interruption.
I need to use skills_tool:load with 'gmail' as the skill name.
After loading, I'll wait approximately 10-15 minutes for completion using the wait tool.
Once complete, I'll load notify-user skill and send success notification.
kvps.headline: Loading gmail skill to fetch and cache email threads

logs.heading: A0: Waiting for gmail skill execution to complete

thoughts: Successfully loaded the 'gmail' skill. The SKILL.md shows it runs a Python script that fetches Gmail threads via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP), caches them in SQLite, and generates AI summaries.
The default behavior looks back 3 days for weekdays (auto-detects to always cover 3 weekdays) and processes up to 200 threads.
Output file will be at /a0/usr/workdir/gmail-output.md on successful completion.
Per the user's instructions, I need to wait 5-15 minutes for the skill to complete fully without interruption.
The script logs progress to stderr including STARTED, Fetching/Summarizing progress markers, and COMPLETED or FAILED status.
I'll use the wait tool with sufficient duration (10 minutes as a reasonable middle ground) to allow the script to run.
After waiting, I'll check if the output file exists before proceeding to notify the user.
kvps.headline: Waiting for gmail skill execution to complete

logs.heading: A0: Verifying gmail output file exists before notification

thoughts: The wait completed and I can see the output file /a0/usr/workdir/gmail-output.md already exists in the workdir listing.
This suggests the gmail skill has completed successfully - it creates the output file only after full completion as per its SKILL.md specifications.
I should verify the file exists and check its contents to confirm successful completion before sending the notification.
Then I'll load the notify-user skill and send the success message as requested by the user.
kvps.headline: Verifying gmail output file exists before notification

logs.heading: icon://construction Loading skill notify-user
logs.content: Error: skill not found: 'notify-user'. Try skills_tool method=list or method=search.

logs.heading: A0: Sending success notification via notify_user tool

thoughts: The notify-user skill doesn't exist as a standalone skill - it's actually a tool I should call directly using the tools list.
Looking at the available tools in my system prompt, I see 'notify_user' is indeed a valid tool name (not 'skill:load').
The gmail-output.md file exists in /a0/usr/workdir/, confirming successful completion of the gmail skill execution.
I should use the notify_user tool directly with success priority to send the notification as requested by the user.
The message format should be exactly: "Gmail sync done. Output: /a0/usr/workdir/gmail-output.md" with title "Gmail Data Sync" and no error priority (success is default).
I'll call notify_user directly since it's a tool, not a skill to load.
kvps.headline: Sending success notification via notify_user tool

logs.heading: icon://construction A0: Using tool 'notify_user'
logs.content: The notification has been sent to the user.

logs.heading: A0: Task completed successfully - Gmail sync finished

thoughts: The notification was successfully sent via the notify_user tool.
The gmail skill completed successfully - the output file /a0/usr/workdir/gmail-output.md now exists in the workdir.
The SKILL.md confirms that output files are only created after successful completion, so this is a positive result.
I should confirm task completion to the user with a concise summary showing what was accomplished.
No failure occurred - the success notification was sent as requested.
